titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/96

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=710 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:16:46.702094+00 | translation moa=711 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:16:49.070754+00

div.p:
. W’ . dei gratia Rex scott’ . Episcopus . abbatibus . comitibus . baronibus . Iusticiis . vicecomitibus . prepositis . et Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . clericis
et laicis salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri me dedisse et hac presenti carta mea confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros . et monachis de
Rieualle ibidem deo seruientibus pro animabus patris mei et matris mee . et Regis dauid aui mei et pro salute anime mee . et omnium fratrum et sororum et antecessorum et suc
cessorum meorum in liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam totam landam de blemeslei per diuisas nominatas in carta Ricardi de Moreuilla . ad aran
dam et colendam sicut eis melius et utilius uisum fuerit . sine sarto faciendo . et licentiam faciendi edificia sua ubi uoluerint super riuulum qui est
inter blaineslei et Milcheside . et totam terram de Milcheside quam Ricardus de Moreuilla et heres eius eis dederunt et cartis suis confirmauerunt per
easdem diuisas que in cartis eorum continentur . et capellam de parco sicut in cartis eisdem continetur . et edificia de cumbesleia et de buchelm et de
Witheleia sicut fuerunt ad festum sancti Michaelis proximum post primam coronationem Ricardi filii Henrici regis anglie . ita quod non ampliora
aut plura edificia ibi faciant quam tunc temporis ibi fuerunt nisi per licentiam meam . Et ut nullus forestariorum meorum det uel uendat boscum
infra diuisas eorum que in mea carta continentur . Hec autem omnia do et concedo eis perpetuo tenore possidere . et hac carta mea confirmo
in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam . et uolo et precipio ut omnia supradicta habeant et possideant ita libere et quiete plenarie et honori
fice sicut ulla elemosina liberius et quietius . plenius et honorificentius haberi potest et possideri a monachis ordinis cisterciensis . Testibus Iocelino episcopo Glasguensi . Arnaldo abbate de Rieualle . hugone cancellario meo . Roberto capellano . meo . Phillippo de Valloniis . Roberto de londoniis
Willelmo de lindesei . Malcolmo filio Comitis Dunecani . Willelmo Cumin Geruasio auenel . Willelmo de haia . Adam filio Gill’ . Adam de sireis . Adam
de stanfort . Herberto Marescallo . apud trauerquer .

William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have given and by this my present charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks of Rievaulx serving God there, for the souls of my father and my mother, and of King David my grandfather, and for my own soul, and of all my brothers and sisters, my ancestors and successors, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, the enitre land of Blainslie, by the boundaries named in the charter of Richard de Morville, for ploughing and cultivating just as it shall seem best and most useful to them, without performing assart, and permission for constructing their own buildings wherever they wish on the burn which is between Blainslie and Milsie, and the entire land of Milsie which Richard de Morville and his heir gave to them, and made firm by their charters, by the same boundaries which are contained in their charters, and the chapel of Park just as it is contained in their charters, and the building at Colmslie and at Buckholm and at Whitelee, just as they were at the feast of Saint Michael next after the first coronation of Richard, son of Henry king of England, provided no enlargement or more buildings are built there than were theere at that time unless by my permission. And so that none of my foresters give or sell wood within their boundaries specified in my charter, I give and grant all these things to them to possess in everlasting tenure, and by this my charter make firm in free and everlasting alm, and I will and instruct that they have and possess all things mentioned above as freely and quitly, fully and honourably, as any alms can most freely and quitly, fully and honourably be had and possessed by monks of the Cistercian order. As witnesses, Jocelin bishop of Glasgow, Arnold abbot of Rievaulx, Hugh my chancellor, Robert my chaplain, Philip de Valognes, Robert of London, William of Lindsay, Malcolm son of Earl Duncan, William Comyn, Gervase Avenel, William de la Hay, Adam son of Gilbert, Adam of Ceres, Adam of Stanford, Herbert the marischal. At Traqhuair.
